sides.label: Common Belief
sides.content: The Ganga is so holy that it can never truly be polluted.

sides.label: Reality
sides.content: The spiritual reverence for the river is powerful, but the physical water is still vulnerable to pollution from sewage, chemicals, and waste.

sides.label: Common Belief
sides.content: Rivers naturally clean themselves no matter how much waste enters them.

sides.label: Reality
sides.content: Rivers do have natural cleaning processes, but excessive pollution overwhelms these systems and turns water toxic.

sides.label: Common Belief
sides.content: If a river is sacred, bathing in it will always be safe.

sides.label: Reality
sides.content: When pollution levels become too high, harmful bacteria and chemicals can make the water unsafe even in sacred locations.
